task_completion.description: GIMP is open with the test image loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible, displaying an image (either a photo or a blue-yellow gradient) in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Layers dockable dialog is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel is open on the screen, showing the current active layer of the image.

task_completion.description: The 'Add Layer Mask' dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Add Layer Mask' or similar is visible on the screen, usually invoked via the Layer menu or right-clicking the layer.

task_completion.description: The 'White (full opacity)' option is selected for the mask initialization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'Add Layer Mask' dialog, the radio button for 'White (full opacity)' is actively selected before the user clicks 'Add'.

task_completion.description: The white layer mask is successfully added to the current layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Layers dialog, the active layer now has a secondary white thumbnail (the mask) immediately to the right of the primary image thumbnail.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GIMP GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor navigating GIMP menus (e.g., Layer -> Mask -> Add Layer Mask) or right-click context menus, rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing ImageMagick commands or GIMP script-fu/python-fu commands being executed to add the mask.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches standard GIMP UI elements, dialogs, and icons, confirming the agent didn't use a different program or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The test image is either a real photo from Unsplash (600x366 or 600x400) or a blue-yellow gradient fallback generated by ImageMagick. The task requires adding a layer mask initialized to 'White (full opacity)'. Successful completion is visually confirmed in GIMP's Layers dialog, where a solid white rectangle (the mask thumbnail) will appear directly next to the layer's image thumbnail.
